rationale: Primary-path inversion confirms unbounded growth: (1) `parseDateRange` at src/gateway/server-methods/usage.ts:222-252 uses `todayStartMs = getTodayStartMs(now, ...)` for both (a) the default last-30-days path and (b) the `days`-only path, so cacheKey strictly rolls each calendar day. Even when UI supplies explicit `startDate/endDate` (today/today by default per ui/src/ui/app.ts:348-355), those dates roll daily as well. (2) Additional cardinality axes: `utcOffset` (any of ~50 validated zones), `mode` in {utc, gateway, specific}, arbitrary operator-picked ranges. Upper bound on distinct (startMs,endMs) is therefore N_days * N_custom_ranges * N_tz, strictly monotonic with operator uptime and interaction. (3) TTL on read is NOT effective eviction: L310 only gates whether stale-but-present value is returned; on miss after TTL expiry, L328 `set(cacheKey, { summary, updatedAt })` overwrites the SAME key, never delete. The Map entry for any previously requested (startMs,endMs) persists for process lifetime. (4) Sibling caches in the same subsystem all implement write-path FIFO eviction: `sessionTitleFieldsCache` (session-utils.fs.ts:63-68 while-loop FIFO + MAX 5000), `resolvedSessionKeyByRunId` (server-session-key.ts:23-28 oldest-first + LIMIT), `TRANSCRIPT_SESSION_KEY_CACHE` (session-transcript-key.ts:142-147 FIFO + MAX 256). costUsageCache lacks any such path. (5) No config-reload, server-maintenance, or graceful-shutdown cleanup touches this Map — grep `costUsageCache|COST_USAGE` yields only the definition file, its test, and an unrelated macos swift file. (6) Hot path: `usage.cost`/`sessions.usage` are UI-driven (usage tab) and CLI `openclaw gateway usage-cost`, not WS hot-path. Call frequency is low (tab switches, manual CLI), so accumulation rate is ~1–10 keys/day per operator at most. Severity caveat (devil side): entry cardinality grows slowly and `CostUsageSummary` payload is bounded by session count — for typical deployments, 1 year of drift is tens-to-hundreds of KB, not OOM-class. P3 is reasonable; P4 could be justified if session volume is small. Pattern is genuine and fix (MAX + FIFO in write-path, mirroring sessionTitleFieldsCache) is local and low-risk, but caller should resist scope creep — this is NOT urgent.

hidden_guards_found: TTL-on-read at L310 — NOT effective eviction (same-key overwrite on refresh, no delete)
__test.costUsageCache.clear() at L365 — test-only reset, never hit in production
sessionTitleFieldsCache FIFO+MAX 5000 at session-utils.fs.ts:63-68 — sibling pattern, confirms absence here is unintended
No config-reload hook clears costUsageCache (grep clean in src/gateway/config-reload.ts)
parseDateRange default (last 30 days) and `days`-only branch both depend on todayStartMs → roll daily; explicit startDate/endDate branch also rolls daily when UI default is today-today
role_specific.primary_path_inversion_result: For 'no accumulation' to hold, all calls would need to share (startMs,endMs) AND UTC offset AND mode interpretation. todayStartMs(now) rolls at midnight for the caller's interpretation, and UI default is today-today (app.ts:348-355), so every calendar day the key changes. Inversion FAILS — the no-accumulation premise is not satisfied by any realistic operator usage. Rate is ~1-10 new keys/day, not cardinality-bounded.

rationale: upstream/main 의 src/gateway/server-methods/usage.ts 는 CAND-014 가 지적한 상태 그대로다. `const costUsageCache = new Map<string, CostUsageCacheEntry>()` (L65) 유지, `loadCostUsageSummaryCached` (L302-352) 의 set 경로 3곳 모두 여전히 평문 Map.set, production delete/prune/evict 부재. 최근 4-6주 내 해당 파일 commit 은 모두 refactor/lint (4fa961d4f1 map spread rule, 66385670e4 dynamic imports, 3ff56020b1 dedupe trimmed readers) 이며 costUsageCache 주소 문제를 건드리지 않음. 즉 CAL-004 식 '상류 이미 merge' 시나리오 아님 → upstream-duplicate 아님. 단 주의: 이전 개발자(evan966890)가 2026-03-05 PR #36682 'perf(usage): bound cost summary cache size' 로 실질적으로 동일 fix (cap=64 + Map 재삽입 LRU, Greptile 5/5) 를 제출했으나 **2026-03-06 merge 없이 CLOSED** (author 자발 close). 또 JuniperTheDev 의 PR #56318 (2026-03-28, OPEN, DIRTY) 이 LRU cap 100 을 포함한 6-in-1 bundled fix 로 살아 있으나 mergeStateStatus DIRTY + size L + one-thing-per-PR 위반이라 merge 전망 낮음. 두 PR 다 현재 upstream HEAD 에 반영 안 됨 → 문제는 여전히 real, fix 도 유효.

upstream_current_state.verdict: FIND-gateway-memory-001 의 R-3 Grep 결과가 upstream HEAD 에서도 동일 — 프로덕션 delete/prune/evict 경로 0개. CAND-014 의 문제 진단은 현재 upstream 에서도 재현 가능.

role_specific.cal004_risk_assessment:
CAL-004 (CAND-005 처럼 upstream 이 이미 merge 한 중복) 시나리오는 아님 — upstream HEAD 에 여전히 bug 존재. 다만 유사하지만 다른 위험 유형 2가지 식별:
(a) '기존 closed PR 중복' 위험: PR #36682 가 2026-03-06 에 essentially 동일 fix 를 제출했다가 close. maintainer 가 stale/이미-제안-됨 이유로 내 PR 을 즉시 close 할 수 있음. 대응: PR 본문에 '#36682 는 author 가 merge 전에 self-closed. 본 PR 은 동일 문제에 대한 재제출이며, 가장 작은 scope 의 XS 사이즈로 재구성' 명시.
(b) '큰 bundled PR 에 포함됨' 위험: PR #56318 이 OPEN 상태로 같은 fix 포함. maintainer 가 'already covered by #56318' 로 reject 가능. 대응: '#56318 은 6개 fix bundled + DIRTY + L size. 본 PR 은 costUsageCache 만 XS 로 분리. one-thing-per-PR 원칙 준수' 명시.
두 가지 모두 R-8 (upstream 최신성 확인) 통과는 OK (upstream main 이 아직 fix 안 됨), 그러나 R-8 를 'merged' 만 체크하면 충분하지 않고 'closed-without-merge' 및 'open-pending' 도 함께 체크해야 함. CAL-004 의 D 절 '페르소나 R-8' 을 'upstream-PR-space' 까지 확장해야 함.
role_specific.recommendation:
proceed — single-CAND 수준에서는 real bug 이며 upstream 에 fix 가 없다. 단 SOL / PR 단계에서 반드시 다음을 수행:
1) PR 본문 'Prior art' 섹션에 PR #36682 (closed) 와 PR #56318 (open, bundled) 을 명시적으로 reference. 각각에 대해 '왜 내 PR 이 이들을 대체/보완하는가' 설명.
2) scope 은 costUsageCache 만 — XS 유지. FIND-002/003 (nodeWakeById, agentRunStarts) 는 별 PR.
3) fix 형태는 PR #36682 의 setCostUsageCacheEntry + delete-then-reinsert LRU 패턴이 same-file 의 sessionTitleFieldsCache 패턴과도 일치하므로 이를 채택. MAX_COST_USAGE_CACHE_ENTRIES 값은 64 (PR #36682 기준) 또는 sessionTitleFieldsCache 의 MAX 값과 맞춤.
4) PR 제출 직전 다시 `git fetch upstream main` + `gh pr list --state all --search costUsageCache` 재실행 (CAL-004 checklist).
5) CAL-005 후속 calibration 으로 'closed-without-merge PR 및 open bundled PR 도 dedup 범위에 포함' 규칙 수립 제안.

rationale: Production hot-path actually exercises the growth scenario without any mocking. macOS app (apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/MenuSessionsInjector.swift:36,75,128 and UsageCostData.swift:134) polls `usage.cost` with `params: nil` every 45s whenever the menu is open, and on every control-channel reconnect. `params: nil` hits `parseDateRange({})` default branch (usage.ts:249-251) which derives `(startMs, endMs)` from `getTodayStartMs(now, {mode: 'utc'})`. At UTC 00:00 `todayStartMs` rotates → new cacheKey, old entry stays in the Map forever (no prune/evict path exists — grep confirmed). Additionally UI controller (ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.ts:184-197) sends startDate/endDate + timezone params chosen by operator; each distinct combination is a new cacheKey. This is the `no-cleanup` R-5 class — unlike CAND-006 which required forcing an edge branch (`process.kill` throw) the test path is literally the production default path. CAL-003/R-7 passes cleanly: reproduction test would just advance the clock a day, call twice, observe `__test.costUsageCache.size === 2` with no mocks. HOWEVER — entry size per FIND is overstated. `CostUsageSummary` (src/infra/session-cost-usage.types.ts:58-63) is daily-aggregated (`daily: CostUsageDailyEntry[]` ≈ 30 entries × ~10 numeric fields), NOT per-session as the FIND's impact_detail claims. Real entry size ≈ 1-2 KB. 1-year macOS-only operator ≈ ~365 entries × ~2 KB ≈ ~0.7 MB. With UI dashboard + timezone toggles: 2-4x. So the leak is REAL and REPRODUCIBLE on the production hot-path, but the magnitude is P3-borderline/P4 rather than the 'tens of MB' the FIND hypothesizes. Fix direction (same-file `sessionTitleFieldsCache` FIFO+cap pattern) is concrete and precedented.

role_specific.production_trigger: macOS status-menu app polls `usage.cost` with params=nil every 45s while menu is open and on every control-channel state change (MenuSessionsInjector:75,128). params=nil → parseDateRange default branch → (startMs, endMs) derived from `Date.UTC(today)` which rotates at UTC 00:00. Every day the cacheKey changes, the old entry remains orphaned (no delete path — rg 'costUsageCache.(delete|clear)' returns only __test-only clear + same-key overwrite). Control UI adds further fan-out via user-selected startDate/endDate/utcTimeZone params (ui/controllers/usage.ts:184-197). Reproduction needs no branch-forcing: advance-clock + 2 calls + assert size==2 is sufficient.
role_specific.cal003_parallel: CAL-003 failure mode was: test mocked `process.kill` throw to force a catch branch that production never entered because a SIGUSR1 listener was always registered. CAND-014 is the opposite — the leaked branch IS the production default. No mock required. `todayStartMs` rotation is real calendar behavior, not a simulated fault. The `__test.costUsageCache.clear()` export is only for test isolation (usage.test.ts:27), so if anything it CONFIRMS the authors knew there was no production reset. R-7 hot-path consistency check passes.
role_specific.recommendation: Proceed to SOL with two calibrations: (1) Correct impact_detail in FIND — CostUsageSummary is daily-aggregated not per-session, so annual heap delta is ~0.5-2 MB per operator not 'tens of MB'. Re-evaluate severity: P3 is defensible if gateway uptime assumption (weeks-months) holds, but P4 is also reasonable. (2) Reproduction test can be maximally straightforward: `vi.useFakeTimers()` → call `loadCostUsageSummaryCached` → advance 25h → call again with same user-facing defaults → assert `__test.costUsageCache.size === 2`. No error-injection, no mock of internals beyond date. Fix template: mirror `sessionTitleFieldsCache` (same file, L63-69) — MAX cap + while-loop FIFO evict on set, optionally prune-on-set entries older than TTL.

rationale: costUsageCache (src/gateway/server-methods/usage.ts:65) is a module-level Map<string, CostUsageCacheEntry> with cacheKey = `${startMs}-${endMs}`. Source inspection confirms: (a) production code has NO delete/evict/prune path — rg for costUsageCache.(delete|clear|evict|splice|shift|pop) yields 0 production matches; only __test.costUsageCache (line 365) and usage.test.ts:27 match, both test-only; (b) TTL(30_000ms) at L310 is read-path only and merely skips stale reads, never removes entries; (c) set operations at L328/L341/L346 only overwrite same key — they don't bound size. The key-space expands with time: parseDateRange derives startMs from getTodayStartMs(now, ...), producing a fresh (startMs, endMs) pair every calendar day, plus combinatorial expansion from `days` option (1/7/30) x utcOffset. Each stale entry retains a CostUsageSummary (session-level token/cost aggregate) — structurally heavy. This matches memory-growth taxonomy of the maintainer's 'core: memory' priority. Crucially, the FIX is already proven in-repo: three sibling caches in the same gateway surface already implement exactly the pattern the fix would apply — resolvedSessionKeyByRunId (server-session-key.ts:23-30, MAX + keys().next() FIFO), TRANSCRIPT_SESSION_KEY_CACHE (session-transcript-key.ts:141-150, same FIFO with MAX=256, even carrying the comment 'Evict oldest-inserted entry when cache exceeds size cap (FIFO bound)'), and sessionTitleFieldsCache (session-utils.fs.ts:28-69, MAX=5000 + while-loop FIFO + LRU bump). The fix is a 6-10 line copy of an idiomatic repo pattern into one cache — one-thing-per-PR compliant, XS-sized, carries no architectural risk. R-5 classification is unambiguous: 'no-cleanup' (write-path has no size cap/prune at all), not a conditional-dependence case.

production_impact_scenarios: S1 (baseline operator): Control UI operator opens usage dashboard daily. parseDateRange uses getTodayStartMs — each calendar day yields a new cacheKey. Over 30 days: Map.size >= 30 stale entries. Over 1 year: several hundred, each holding a CostUsageSummary (per-session token/cost aggregate). No major GC reclaim — GC-hostile due to Map retention.

S2 (dashboard with range switcher): UI exposes multiple `days` options (1/7/30). Each switch at a given timestamp produces a distinct (startMs, endMs) pair. Combined with daily todayStartMs rotation: multiplicative key-space growth. operator_days x range_options = 30 days x 3 options = 90 entries/month for a single operator.

S3 (multi-timezone / utcOffset): parseDateRange includes utcOffsetMinutes in interpretation. Operators traveling, daylight-saving boundaries, or multi-region usage produces further key distinctness — not self-bounding.
S4 (automated polling client): any scripted monitor that hits usage.cost with rolling date params amplifies the rate. No rate limiter on cache population.

S5 (long-uptime launchd/systemd gateway): macOS gateway runs as LaunchAgent (`ai.openclaw.gateway` per /Users/lucas/Project/openclaw/CLAUDE.md) — uptimes measured in weeks-to-months between releases. No process restart cadence bounds leak. Graceful shutdown / config-reload paths do not clear the Map.
role_specific.maintainer_receptiveness: Aligns directly with explicit maintainer priorities quoted in /Users/lucas/Project/openclaw-audit/CLAUDE.md: 'Our focus right now is bugs, critical issues, reliability and stability... Where we need the most help: Core issues like memory, plugin loading, cron, reliability.' This is a core gateway memory leak, not a feature, not a refactor, not a CI change. Additional maintainer-preference alignment: (a) one-thing-per-PR — single file, single cache, ~6-10 LOC diff; (b) XS target size easily achievable; (c) no @ts-nocheck or lint suppression needed; (d) no CODEOWNERS restricted path (src/gateway/server-methods/usage.ts is not in the restricted list — /src/cron/service/jobs.ts, /src/cron/stagger.ts, /src/agents/*auth*, /src/agents/sandbox* per the audit CLAUDE.md); (e) pattern already accepted by maintainers three times in sibling files, so no architectural debate needed — this is strictly consistency-closing. Prompt-cache-stability guidance in /Users/lucas/Project/openclaw/CLAUDE.md does not apply (this cache is not a model payload). Gateway CLAUDE.md guardrails (don't materialize bundled plugin runtime in Gateway HTTP) also don't apply — the change stays within server-methods. Receptiveness assessment: HIGH.
role_specific.reproducibility: Fully reproducible without live keys. Test recipe: (1) import __test.costUsageCache from src/gateway/server-methods/usage.ts and clear; (2) call __test.loadCostUsageSummaryCached N times with monotonically varying (startMs, endMs) pairs (e.g. loop i=0..999 with startMs = base + i*DAY_MS); (3) after each successful resolution expect __test.costUsageCache.size to equal i+1 — no ceiling. Regression test after fix: expect size to plateau at the configured MAX. __test export at L355-366 is precisely the seam for this test; usage.test.ts already uses it at line 27. No external IO, no model calls, no fs mocks required beyond stubbing loadCostUsageSummary. Reproduction requires zero production deployment — purely unit-level.
concerns_acknowledged: CostUsageSummary per-entry size is inferred (10-100KB estimate), not profiled. If sessions are small, the leak is slow enough to be P4 rather than P3. FIND self-check acknowledges this.

config-reload path was not traced — if config reload clears this Map, absolute impact is lower (but the module-level Map is not referenced by config-reload machinery based on the grep surface). Even if reload cleared it, long-uptime gateways between reloads still leak.

Severity P3 (not P2) is correct and conservative — this is a slow leak, not an immediate OOM. Maintainer might reasonably triage lower than P2 bug fixes. Positive advocate should not oversell severity.
Entry count is bounded by the combinatorial key-space rather than by attacker control — so this is not DoS-grade; correctly classified as slow leak.

Sibling-fix framing is strong but not a guarantee of merge — maintainer acceptance is probabilistic per their 'cant GUARANTEE we will merge' disclaimer. The case remains strongly mergeable on technical merits.

rationale: costUsageCache is read/written only by loadCostUsageSummaryCached (usage.ts:302-352), invoked on every usage.cost RPC handler (usage.ts:384). The cache is module-scoped, no production delete/prune/evict/clear path exists (Grep confirmed: only test-only __test.costUsageCache.clear at L365; all .set/.get sites are inside the cached loader). Each request runs parseDateRange which derives startMs from getTodayStartMs(now, interpretation) (usage.ts:231) — the key space monotonically grows by at least one new entry per calendar day whenever dashboards/CLI poll usage.cost (UI calls it alongside sessions.usage in a Promise.all on each dashboard refresh; see ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.ts:184-197). Key generation rate per request: 1 key per (startMs,endMs,utcOffset) tuple — within TTL 30s the same tuple overwrites in place, but day rollover, days param change, and utcOffset variants all produce fresh non-recoverable keys. Same file has correct FIFO eviction on other caches (resolvedSessionKeyByRunId L22-30, sessionTitleFieldsCache L63-69) confirming this is a pattern regression, not intentional. The proposed fix (MAX + FIFO evict on set, modeled on sessionTitleFieldsCache) matches how the sibling caches in the same file are already implemented; fix is well-scoped and single-file. Confidence is medium (not high) because: (1) hot-path pressure depends on operator UI poll cadence, not well-quantified — slow leak over months, not hours; (2) CostUsageSummary size is session-count dependent, so absolute heap impact is extrapolation; (3) graceful-shutdown and config-reload paths were grep-verified to not touch the Map, so process lifetime bounds the leak.

callers_of_target.cacheability: per-request miss unless within TTL 30s of prior identical (startMs,endMs)

callers_of_target.key_generation: parseDateRange({startDate,endDate,days,mode,utcOffset}) — todayStartMs advances daily; days/utcOffset/startDate/endDate params all widen key space

rpc_entry_callers: ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.ts:192 — client.request("usage.cost", ...) invoked inside loadUsage() alongside sessions.usage in Promise.all; triggered on dashboard load and user range changes

caching_per_request.within_ttl_same_key: hit — overwrite in place, no new entry
caching_per_request.outside_ttl_same_key: miss — re-fetch, overwrite same key (still one entry)
caching_per_request.new_key: miss — NEW ENTRY ADDED; never evicted (no delete path)

key_generation_rate_per_day.floor: 1 new key/day (todayStartMs rollover with default 30-day range)
key_generation_rate_per_day.typical: 3-10 new keys/day (operator toggles days=1/7/30, possibly utcOffset variants)
key_generation_rate_per_day.ceiling_per_year: ~1000-3000 distinct keys for long-running single operator, matching FIND estimate

role_specific.classification: low-RPS-but-monotonic-key-growth operator-control-plane path; not request-hot but cumulative-unbounded. Map.size grows O(days_elapsed * range_options * utc_variants). Each entry holds CostUsageSummary (per-session agg) which is session-count dependent — 10-100KB per entry typical.
role_specific.recommendation: Accept candidate for SOL. Fix pattern = copy sessionTitleFieldsCache L63-69 approach (MAX_COST_USAGE_CACHE_ENTRIES + while-loop FIFO evict on set at L328 and L346; optional TTL prune-on-set to drop entries past 2*TTL). P3 severity consistent with slow leak — operator dashboard path, not per-message hot path. Single-file fix, same-file precedent for FIFO, aligns with openclaw maintainer focus (memory/reliability). Upstream-duplicate check not flagged: no existing issue referenced in CAND, no comment 'intentional unbounded' in source. Greenlight for solution-architect.
